summary_one_line: Input must carry the 4-byte FeatureParams tag literal 'size' (73 69 7a 65) so tag == HB_TAG('s','i','z','e') is true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 4-byte feature-record tag equal to the ASCII literal 'size' (73 69 7a 65) reaching the FeatureParams::sanitize tag dispatch, satisfying the exact 32-bit equality tag == HB_TAG('s','i','z','e').
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed (value_profile_cmplog) contains the byte run 73 69 7a 65 ('size') at offset 0x39, so the true branch at line 688 is taken and resolution occurs; the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that are Hamming-closer to the 'size' constant, steering the corpus toward the full 4-byte match.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking cmplog seeds carry 'GSUB'/'GPOS' tags (47 53 55 42 / 47 50 4f 53) but never the 'size' tag; at the blocker the loser side takes the false branch (T=0 F=54) on every reached execution, so without the gradient signal cmplog stalls before assembling the exact 'size' constant.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers already have I2S (cmplog); the only added technique in the winner is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback, which buckets partial-match (Hamming/prefix) distance to the compared constant as new coverage, giving an intermediate climbing signal toward the 32-bit 'size' equality that bare cmplog lacks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the equality test tag == HB_TAG('s','i','z','e') whose true side is globally blocked.
The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating cmplog, the differentiating technique being value_profile.
The winner-resolving seed was discovered by value_profile_cmplog and contains the literal byte run 'size'.
The winner takes the true branch at the blocker (resolved) while the loser blocks, taking the false side on every reached execution.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 688  T=2 F=0  T=0 F=54  if (tag == HB_TAG ('s','i','z','e'))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser-blocking cmplog seeds carry GSUB/GPOS tags rather than the size tag.
The winner adds value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog's CMP buffer and edge counters.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: The taken true branch leads to size sanitization, the resolving side reached only by the winner.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage not required: the decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog > cmplog with delta value_profile, so both arms already have cmplog/I2S and the differentiating technique is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient, not I2SRandReplace. The dash-row floor check applies only to I2S claims; here the gradient feedback (not a mutator) is the attributed mechanism.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a 4-byte exact tag equality where value_profile_cmplog shows no advantage over cmplog (equal or slower time-to-resolve), which would refute that the CMP_MAP gradient is the differentiator and point back to a shared I2S find.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is shown, so the claim that the value_profile gradient (rather than a lucky shared-cmplog havoc find) drove the win rests on the prob_div and the single 'size'-bearing seed.
